page: Amazon Web Services x8g.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by 96 dedicated physical cores of the ARM64 AWS Neoverse-V2 processor. Operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz, this processor features 64 KB L1d cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 36 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per core. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage and network interfaces with a baseline bandwidth of 40 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance and memory throughput. The stress-ng multi-core CPU test, bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, and Geekbench multi-core scores all rank in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, with cached read-only small block speeds reaching 11096.57 GB/sec and uncached large block read speeds at 481.49 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average and strong categories, respectively. Additionally, text generation for small language models registers in the bottom 10%, though larger model text generation performs in the top 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture provides high resource density and memory capacity without the overhead of hyperthreading, as each of the 96 cores is a dedicated physical unit. The absence of bundled local storage and GPUs reduces baseline hardware costs for memory-bound workloads that do not require acceleration. This hardware profile is highly suitable for large-scale in-memory databases, Redis deployments, heavy database operations, and multi-threaded software compilation.
description: Amazon Web Services x8g.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring 96 dedicated physical ARM64 cores on the 2.8 GHz AWS Neoverse-V2 processor. It is equipped with 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory, providing 16.0 GB of RAM per core, but includes no local storage or GPUs. The server delivers top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, with benchmarks like stress-ng multi-core, Redis SET operations, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression ranking in the top 10%. Conversely, single-core metrics and small-model text generation are comparatively moderate. Given its high memory density and dedicated physical cores, this server is designed for memory-intensive workloads, including large-scale database operations, Redis caching layers, and multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x8g.metal-24xl is a bare-metal ARM64 server with 96 dedicated Neoverse-V2 cores, 1.5 TB of DDR5 memory, and 40 Gbps network bandwidth, built for memory-intensive database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x8g.metal-24xl is a bare-metal ARM64 server featuring 96 dedicated physical cores and 1536 GB of DDR5 memory.
tagline: A bare-metal ARM64 platform delivering 1.5 TB of DDR5 memory and 96 dedicated physical cores for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated physical ARM64 AWS Neoverse-V2 cores operating at 2.8 GHz
1536.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory providing 16.0 GB per core
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth in benchmark testing
40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for large-scale in-memory databases, Redis, and multi-threaded compilation
